blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Hypertensive Disorders of Pregnancy?
Preeclampsia คือ leading cause ของ indicated preterm delivery ทั่วโลก — 15–20% of all preterm births attributable to preeclampsia (ACOG PB 202, 2019). สิ่งที่ neonatologist ต้องรู้: (1) MgSO₄ ผ่าน placenta → neonatal respiratory depression + poor tone — เตรียม team ในห้องคลอดเสมอ; (2) 12–25% FGR attributable to preeclampsia → เตรียม AEDV/REDV feeding strategy; (3) Antenatal corticosteroids: betamethasone 12 mg IM x2 doses — timing จาก OB สำคัญต่อ lung maturity baby; (4) HELLP syndrome → thrombocytopenia → ↑ IVH risk ใน preterm neonate; (5) MgSO₄ ≥34 wk neuroprotection → ↓ cerebral palsy ใน surviving preterm (Doyle Cochrane 2009).
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
จำแนก 4 categories ของ hypertensive disorders of pregnancy พร้อม diagnostic criteria แต่ละชนิด
อธิบาย 2-stage pathophysiology ของ preeclampsia: incomplete spiral artery remodeling → maternal vascular dysfunction
ระบุ angiogenic biomarkers: sFlt-1, PlGF, soluble endoglin — บทบาทใน pathogenesis และ prediction
ระบุ risk factors ของ preeclampsia: pregnancy-specific vs maternal pre-existing; ความสำคัญเชิง clinical
อธิบาย organ-specific pathophysiology: cardiovascular, renal, cerebral, hepatic, placental
วางแผนจัดการ: corticosteroids, MgSO₄ (dose/duration/toxicity/neonatal effects), antihypertensives (labetalol/hydralazine/nifedipine)
กำหนด delivery timing: ตาม gestational age + severe features + ACOG algorithm (Fig 16.2)
วินิจฉัย HELLP syndrome และแยกแยะจาก acute fatty liver, TTP, HUS — treatment ต่างกันอย่างไร
ระบุ chronic hypertension: CHAP trial findings (Tita NEJM 2022); antihypertensive agents ที่ safe ใน pregnancy
อธิบาย prevention: aspirin (USPSTF/ACOG 2021 — dose, timing, indications); calcium; vitamins C+E
เชื่อมสู่ neonatal care: MgSO₄ effects on neonate, FGR management, HELLP thrombocytopenia implications
1. Classification — 4 Categories (ACOG 2019)

Classification ใช้ ACOG Task Force 2013 (revised 2019) — ACOG PB 202
ก่อนปี 2000 ใช้คำว่า 'toxemia' และ 'pregnancy-induced hypertension' — คำเหล่านี้ obsolete แล้ว. ปัจจุบัน 4 categories: (1) Gestational hypertension, (2) Preeclampsia-eclampsia, (3) Chronic hypertension, (4) Superimposed preeclampsia on chronic hypertension. BP ≥140/90 on 2 occasions ≥4 h apart after 20 wk in previously normotensive = diagnostic threshold. BP ≥160/110 = severe range — can confirm within minutes to initiate therapy faster.

rows: BP ≥140/90 on 2 occasions ≥4h apart after 20 wk, previously normotensive; NO proteinuria; resolves by 12 wk postpartum (if not → chronic HTN)
BP ≥140/90 after 20 wk PLUS: proteinuria ≥300 mg/24h (or P:Cr ≥0.3 or dipstick 1+) OR (no proteinuria) + thrombocytopenia <100k, Cr >1.1, LFT 2x ULN, pulmonary edema, or new headache
BP ≥160/110 on 2 occasions ≥4h; OR thrombocytopenia <100k; OR LFT 2x + RUQ pain; OR progressive renal insufficiency; OR pulmonary edema; OR new-onset headache
Generalized seizures in a preeclamptic woman not attributable to other causes
Hemolysis + elevated liver enzymes + low platelets; may occur without HTN or proteinuria; variant of preeclampsia
HTN before pregnancy or diagnosed before 20 wk; OR persists >12 wk postpartum
Sudden ↑ BP in previously controlled chronic HTN; OR new proteinuria; OR end-organ findings: thrombocytopenia, ↑ LFT, rapid ↓ renal function

blocks.text: Note: คำว่า 'mild preeclampsia' ถูกแทนที่ด้วย 'preeclampsia without severe features' — เพื่อเน้น progressive + systemic nature; FGR ถูกเอาออกจาก diagnostic criteria (แต่ยังสำคัญในการ evaluation); edema ไม่ใช่ diagnostic criterion (10–15% normotensive pregnant women มี nondependent edema).
2. Epidemiology
Incidence: 3–10% of all pregnancies; preeclampsia increasing in US — up 25% between 1987–2004 (Wallis Am J Hypertens 2008)
Global maternal mortality: hypertensive disorders = 16% maternal deaths in developed countries; up to 26% in Latin America/Caribbean (Khan WHO 2006)
Eclampsia trend (US): preeclampsia ↑ 25%, but eclampsia ↓ 22% between 1987–2004 — improved management
Neonatal impact: 12–25% FGR + SGA attributable to preeclampsia; 15–20% all preterm births attributable to preeclampsia — indicated (not spontaneous) preterm
In utero: preterm infant complications → neonatal deaths + serious long-term morbidity (see Day 111 for FGR complications)
Recurrence risk: 7–20%; higher with 2 prior preeclamptic pregnancies OR earlier gestational age at onset

Long-term CV risk: 2011 AHA added preeclampsia to cardiovascular disease risk factor list; women with prior preeclampsia: ↑ HTN, dyslipidemia, endothelial dysfunction months–years later (Benschop Heart 2019)
Prematurity + adult health (DOHaD): systematic review 18 studies, 45,249 individuals: ↑ BP + BMI in children/young adults born to preeclamptic pregnancies (Davis Pediatrics 2012)
3. Risk Factors
3.1 Pregnancy-Specific
Nulliparity: ~3x risk; 2/3 of cases occur in first pregnancies
New paternity: limited prior sperm exposure (nulliparity, barrier contraception, ICSI, longer interpregnancy interval) → ↑ risk
Multifetal gestation: excess placental volume → earlier onset + more severe; risk ↑ with each additional fetus
Hydatidiform mole: excess placental volume → preeclampsia may occur before 20 wk
3.2 Maternal Pre-Existing

rows: 20–25% risk superimposed PE in mild; up to 50% in severe prepregnancy HTN (ACOG PB 203, 2019)
2–3x risk overall; risk ↑ even within normal BMI range; early-onset + severe PE also ↑ (Bodnar Epidemiology 2007)
Antiphospholipid antibody → thrombotic vasculopathy; SLE vasculitis → poor placentation
Endothelial + vascular dysfunction; ↑ PE risk correlates with severity
~2x risk after controlling for chronic medical conditions
Higher prevalence + worse outcomes — structural inequity, racism, healthcare access (Johnson Am J Obstet Gynecol 2022)
Cigarette smoking REDUCES risk — mechanism unclear; NOT a reason to allow smoking

blocks.text: 4. Pathophysiology — Two-Stage Model

Two-Stage Concept (Roberts & Gammill Hypertension 2005)
Stage I: inadequate remodeling of maternal spiral arteries by invasive cytotrophoblast → high-resistance uteroplacental circulation → ↓ placental perfusion. Stage II: placental ischemia-hypoxia → release of factors (sFlt-1, soluble endoglin, inflammatory cytokines, microparticles) → systemic maternal vascular dysfunction + endothelial damage → multiorgan involvement. สตรีที่มี pre-existing vascular disease (chronic HTN, DM, SLE) → poor placental implantation (Stage I) AND/OR ↑ susceptibility to vascular damage (Stage II) → ↑ PE risk.
4.1 Angiogenic Factors

sFlt-1 (soluble fms-like tyrosine kinase-1): binds + inactivates VEGF and PlGF → ↓ endothelial function; ↑ in preeclampsia weeks before clinical onset; correlates with severity (Levine NEJM 2004;350:672)
PlGF (placental growth factor): proangiogenic; ↓ in preeclampsia weeks before onset; sFlt-1/PlGF ratio used as biomarker in some countries
Soluble endoglin: inhibits TGF-β1 signaling → disrupts NO-induced vasodilation; potentiates severe PE + HELLP + FGR in animal models (Venkatesha Nature Med 2006)
Placental ischemia + hypoxia hypothesized to trigger sFlt-1 release; rat models of sFlt-1 injection → HTN + proteinuria + glomerular endotheliosis
4.2 Immunologic / Inflammatory Factors
Paternal antigen exposure: ↓ exposure → ↑ PE risk (nulliparity, new paternity, barrier contraception, ICSI) — immunologic tolerance model

HLA-C / NK cell interactions: maternal decidual NK cells (KIRs) interact with fetal trophoblast HLA-C molecules → regulate placental implantation; certain HLA-C + KIR isoforms predispose to PE (Moffett Placenta 2007)
Inflammatory markers ↑: CRP, TNF-α, IL-6 all elevated in PE vs normotensive pregnancy — exaggerated systemic inflammatory state
Placental hypoxia-reperfusion → syncytiotrophoblast apoptosis + necrosis → circulating trophoblastic debris + antiangiogenic microparticles → ↑ maternal inflammation
4.3 RAAS and Endothelial Dysfunction
Angiotensin II type 1 receptor autoantibody: agonistic autoantibody → activates receptor → HTN + vascular injury; injection in rodents → PE features (Dechend 2015)

Endothelial dysfunction: reduced flow-mediated vasodilation; ↑ cellular fibronectin, von Willebrand factor, thrombomodulin; ↑ endothelin-1 + thromboxane; ↓ prostacyclin + nitric oxide; ↑ oxidative stress
4.4 Organ-Specific Pathophysiology

rows: High SVR + HTN (low-output/high-resistance state); heart responds to hemodynamic changes; untreated: low CO + high SVR
GFR ↓ 32% + RBF ↓ 24% vs normal pregnancy; glomerular endotheliosis (endothelial swelling, loss of fenestrations); ↑ urinary protein; ↑ uric acid; resolves post-delivery
Vasospasm + loss of cerebrovascular autoregulation → posterior reversible leukoencephalopathy syndrome (PRES); hemorrhage, edema, infarct; retinal arteriolar narrowing on fundoscopy
Periportal hemorrhage + intraparenchymal infarction; subcapsular hemorrhage → RUQ/epigastric pain (stretching Glisson capsule)
Acute atherosis (fibrinoid necrosis + lipid-laden macrophages); ↑ cytotrophoblast, ↓ syncytiotrophoblast (hypoxia); small placenta + infarcts

blocks.text: 5. Clinical Presentation and Initial Evaluation
5.1 Clinical Presentation
Most preeclampsia diagnosed after 34 wk; ~10% early-onset (<34 wk)
~25% will present with or develop severe features; progression unpredictable — may worsen within hours to days
Postpartum preeclampsia: usually within first 2 wk after delivery — high clinical suspicion needed
Symptoms: headache, visual changes/scotomata, epigastric/RUQ pain, nausea, vomiting, dyspnea, ↓ urine output, ↓ fetal movement
5.2 Initial Evaluation
Serial BP in triage/hospital setting; physical exam including fundoscopy if indicated
Labs: CBC + platelets; LFT (AST, ALT); serum creatinine; ± peripheral smear + LDH + haptoglobin + indirect bilirubin (if HELLP concern)
Proteinuria: 24h urine total protein (gold standard ≥300 mg); OR protein:creatinine ratio ≥0.3; dipstick 1+ only if other methods unavailable
Fetal surveillance: US (EFW, growth, AFI); NST ± BPP; UA Doppler if FGR present
6. Management — Principles

Definitive Treatment คือ Delivery — Balance Maternal vs Fetal Risk
Delivery เสมอ beneficial ต่อแม่ — prevents disease progression + organ damage. แต่ preterm delivery อาจเป็นอันตรายต่อ fetus/neonate. ดังนั้น decision ขึ้นอยู่กับ: gestational age + severity + maternal/fetal status. Key interventions: (1) corticosteroids สำหรับ lung maturation <37 wk; (2) MgSO₄ seizure prophylaxis; (3) antihypertensives สำหรับ severe range BP; (4) fetal surveillance. Multidisciplinary team: MFM + neonatology + critical care + obstetric anesthesia.
6.1 Corticosteroids
Indication: preeclampsia <37 wk at risk for preterm delivery — ↓ RDS, IVH, NEC, mortality
Regimen (NIH consensus): betamethasone 12 mg IM x2 doses 24h apart; IV dexamethasone ถ้ากังวล coagulopathy (avoid deep IM injection)
Late preterm (34 0/7 – 36 6/7 wk): betamethasone shows potential benefit if late preterm delivery anticipated — Gyamfi-Bannerman NEJM 2016;374:1311
6.2 Magnesium Sulfate — Seizure Prophylaxis

MgSO₄: Recommended for Severe Preeclampsia — Neonatal Effects ที่ต้องรู้
Evidence (Duley Cochrane 2010): 6 trials, 11,444 women; MgSO₄ vs placebo → seizure RR 0.41 (95% CI 0.29–0.58) — superior to phenytoin, diazepam, lytic cocktail. Dose: 4g IV loading dose → continuous infusion 1–2 g/h; reduce in renal failure; duration: labor + 24–48h postpartum. Toxicity monitoring: loss of reflexes, respiratory depression. Neonatal effects: MgSO₄ crosses placenta — cord blood Mg ≈ maternal level; neonatal respiratory depression + poor tone + hypothermia — neonatology team ควรอยู่ในห้องคลอด. Neuroprotection: MgSO₄ before anticipated early preterm birth → ↓ CP in surviving infants (Doyle Cochrane 2009) — ACOG recommends for <32 wk especially.
6.3 Antihypertensive Therapy
Goal: prevent cerebrovascular accidents (stroke/ICH) + acute coronary events — NOT tight chronic control
Threshold for acute treatment: SBP >160 mmHg AND/OR DBP >105 mmHg — treat in hospital setting
ACE inhibitors / ARBs: CONTRAINDICATED in pregnancy — 1st trimester: CNS + cardiac anomalies; 2nd/3rd trimester: fetal renal failure, oligohydramnios, pulmonary hypoplasia, skull ossification defects

rows: 10–20 mg IV → 20–80 mg q10–30 min (max 300 mg) OR 1–2 mg/min infusion
5 mg IV/IM → 5–10 mg q20–40 min OR 0.5–10 mg/h infusion
↑ maternal hypotension + fetal distress vs others
10–20 mg PO → repeat in 30 min → q2–6h
Reflex tachycardia; headache; do NOT use sublingual
Last resort; cyanide toxicity with prolonged use; ICU only

blocks.text: 7. Delivery Timing — ACOG Algorithm (Fig. 16.2)
7.1 At or Near Term
≥37 0/7 wk: deliver — neonatal outcomes favorable; continuation = ↑ maternal/fetal risk (Spong Obstet Gynecol 2011)
Without severe features <37 wk: expectant management + close surveillance; deliver if progression to severe disease or non-reassuring maternal/fetal status
7.2 Severe Features

Severe Preeclampsia <34 wk — สามตัวเลือก (Sibai Am J Obstet Gynecol 2011)
(1) Immediate delivery after maternal stabilization: ถ้ามี: uncontrollable severe HTN, eclampsia, pulmonary edema, DIC, new/worsening renal dysfunction, abruption, non-reassuring fetal status. (2) Short-term prolongation 48h for steroid benefit: neurologic/epigastric symptoms, partial HELLP, thrombocytopenia, ↑ LFT, FGR — deliver after 48h if stable. (3) Expectant management (>48h) ถ้า BP controlled, no severe end-organ involvement, reassuring fetal status: RCTs (Odendaal 1990; Sibai 1994): significant pregnancy prolongation + ↓ RDS + ↓ NICU days + ↑ birth weight with reasonable maternal safety. Acceptable only in tertiary center with MFM + NICU + ICU + anesthesia 24/7; deliver at 34 wk or earlier if worsening.
≥34 0/7 wk with severe features: deliver
Mode of delivery: vaginal delivery preferred; cesarean for usual obstetric indications only; vaginal delivery rate ~33% at <28–32 wk (Alexander 1999)
8. HELLP Syndrome
8.1 Diagnosis and Features
Definition: Hemolysis + Elevated Liver enzymes + Low Platelets
Epidemiology: 10–20% of severe preeclampsia-eclampsia; 15–20% of HELLP has NO prior HTN or proteinuria
Symptoms: epigastric/RUQ pain, nausea, vomiting, malaise; sudden + progressive maternal/fetal deterioration
Neonatal concern: thrombocytopenia in mother → neonatal thrombocytopenia in ~20–30% (transient; resolves 2–7 days) → ↑ IVH risk in preterm neonate
Hepatic complications: infarction, hemorrhage, rupture — severe morbidity
Corticosteroids for HELLP: Cochrane meta-analysis: ↑ platelet count but no evidence of improvement in maternal mortality or severe morbidity — additional studies needed
8.2 Differential Diagnosis — HELLP vs Mimics

Key principle: preeclampsia + HELLP + acute fatty liver of pregnancy → resolve soon after delivery; TTP/HUS → delivery does NOT cure; plasmapheresis is mainstay. Proper diagnosis often made only after delivery by following disease progression.
9. Chronic Hypertension in Pregnancy
Prevalence: ~2% all pregnancies; increases with age; higher in Black women
Superimposed PE risk: 20–25% in mild chronic HTN; up to 50% with severe prepregnancy HTN
Perinatal outcomes: perinatal death 29/1000 in uncomplicated chronic HTN vs normotensive; FGR 10.5%; superimposed PE → FGR 35% + perinatal death RR 3.6 (Rey 1994)

CHAP Trial — Tight Control ดีกว่า? (Tita NEJM 2022;387:664)
Design: pragmatic RCT; mild chronic hypertension in pregnancy; tight control (target BP <140/90) vs treat only if BP ≥160/105. Result: tight control group → fewer adverse outcomes with NO increase in SGA birth weight. Current practice: target BP <140/90 using oral labetalol or nifedipine XL as first-line agents. Pre-existing renal disease/retinopathy/cardiac disease → may benefit from tighter control. ACE inhibitors/ARBs: CONTRAINDICATED — start discontinuation even in preconception period.
9.1 Oral Antihypertensives — Safe in Pregnancy

rows: Childhood safety data to 7 yr; lethargy side effect; less effective in severe HTN
Adjunctive only; concern ↓ intravascular volume → ↓ uteroplacental flow
Adjunctive; add to sympatholytic to prevent tachycardia
Teratogenic; fetal renal failure; pulmonary hypoplasia

blocks.text: 10. Prediction and Prevention
10.1 Prediction — No Single Test Recommended (US 2022)
Clinical risk factors alone: detection rate 37% early-onset PE, 29% late-onset PE at 5% FPR (Poon J Hum Hypertens 2010)

Combined first-trimester screening: PlGF + uterine artery pulsatility index + MAP + PAPP-A + BMI + parity + prior PE → 93% detection rate for early-onset PE at 5% FPR (Poon Hypertension 2009) — promising but not yet standard in US
sFlt-1/PlGF ratio: used in some European countries to triage suspected PE; not yet standard in US or Thailand
10.2 Prevention — Aspirin (Best Evidence)

Low-Dose Aspirin — USPSTF 2021 Recommendation
Meta-analysis 74 trials >40,000 women (Duley Cochrane 2019): aspirin → ↓ proteinuric PE, ↓ fetal/neonatal death, ↓ preterm birth <37 wk, ↓ SGA, ↓ composite serious adverse outcomes. USPSTF 2021 (Henderson JAMA 2021;326:1192): aspirin RR 0.85 PE, RR 0.79 perinatal mortality, RR 0.80 preterm birth, RR 0.82 IUGR; no increase in bleeding harms. High-risk indications (ACOG + USPSTF): prior PE, pregestational DM, chronic HTN, multifetal gestation, renal disease, APS/SLE. 2+ moderate-risk factors (nulliparity, obesity, family history PE, age ≥35 yr). Start: ideally 12 wk, MUST be before 16 wk; dose: aspirin 81 mg daily; emerging evidence for 100–162 mg. (Roberge Am J Obstet Gynecol 2017)
10.3 Other Prevention Strategies
Calcium: Cochrane 13 trials 15,730 women: ↓ PE risk (RR 0.45, 95% CI 0.31–0.65) — greatest benefit in low calcium intake populations (RR 0.36); supplement 1.5–2 g/day (Hofmeyr Cochrane 2018)
Vitamins C + E: NOT effective — Cochrane 15 trials >20,000 women: no benefit (RR 0.94, 95% CI 0.82–1.07; Rumbold Cochrane 2008)
Bed rest: limited evidence; NOT recommended; risks include VTE
Exercise: inconclusive — may ↓ PE risk via improved vascular function + placental angiogenesis; larger RCTs needed
11. NICU Practical — ทีม Neonatologist กับ Preeclampsia
ก่อนคลอด: รับ history จาก OB: severe features present? Gestational age? MgSO₄ started? Steroid given (timing + doses)? Fetal status (FGR/Doppler)?

MgSO₄ neonatal effects: cord blood Mg ≈ maternal serum Mg — respiratory depression, poor tone, poor feeding, hypothermia; ถ้าทารกซึม/หายใจช้า → assess need ventilatory support; calcium gluconate (antidote) rarely needed
Corticosteroid timing: betamethasone ✓ ถ้า <34 wk (และพิจารณา 34–36+6 wk); หากให้ครบ 48h แล้ว → ling maturity benefit maximal; antenatal steroids ไม่ครบ = ↑ RDS risk — document ใน admission note

HELLP + thrombocytopenia: maternal HELLP → neonatal thrombocytopenia transient ~20–30% — check CBC day 1; ↑ IVH risk in preterm neonate; platelet <50k พิจารณา transfusion ถ้า symptomatic หรือ procedure planned
Preterm delivery (indicated): prepare NICU based on GA: <28 wk → tertiary readiness; 28–32 wk → surfactant, CPAP, TPN, MgSO₄ neuroprotection
FGR from preeclampsia: follow Day 111 protocol: glucose monitoring, GIR 6–8, temperature, feeding strategy (HM trophic ถ้า AEDV/REDV), NIRS, cranial US

ACEi/ARB exposure: rare: if mother on ACEi in 2nd/3rd trimester (prescribed incorrectly or inadvertently) → evaluate neonate for renal function, oliguria, oligohydramnios effects (pulmonary hypoplasia); renal US; monitor BUN/Cr closely
Postpartum preeclampsia: if mother develops PE postpartum while baby in NICU → briefing ทีม; mother's capacity to breastfeed/bond may be affected; coordinate with obstetric team
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — MgSO₄ ผ่าน Placenta — เตรียม Neonatal Team เสมอเมื่อแม่ได้ MgSO₄
Cord blood Mg ≈ maternal Mg. Neonatal effects: respiratory depression, poor tone, hypothermia, poor feeding. ถ้า maternal loading dose ให้ไม่นานก่อนคลอด → ↑ neonatal level. เตรียม NICU team + อุปกรณ์ positive pressure ventilation. Calcium gluconate คือ antidote แต่ rarely needed; ดูแล ventilation + warming เป็นหลัก.

PEARL 2 — Severe Features ≠ Delivery Immediately ถ้า <34 wk + Stable
Sibai trials (Odendaal 1990; Sibai 1994): expectant management severe PE <34 wk (BP controlled, no end-organ failure, reassuring fetal) → significant pregnancy prolongation + ↓ RDS + ↓ NICU days. แต่ conditions: tertiary center + continuous maternal/fetal monitoring. Preterm baby ที่เกิดที่ 30 wk แทน 28 wk = ↓ morbidity อย่างมีนัยสำคัญ. ดังนั้น: 'delivery' ไม่ใช่คำตอบที่ถูกต้องเสมอไปถ้า <34 wk + stable.

PEARL 3 — HELLP Differential ต้องแยกจาก TTP/HUS — Treatment ต่างกัน 180 องศา
HELLP + acute fatty liver + TTP + HUS ล้วน overlap กัน. Key: TTP/HUS = plasmapheresis; HELLP/AFL = delivery. Delivery ไม่รักษา TTP/HUS. Key discriminator: TTP → ↓ ADAMTS13 + ↑ vWF multimers; neurologic dominant; HUS → renal failure dominant + complement/Shiga toxin; AFL → hypoglycemia + coagulopathy + jaundice. ถ้า delivery แล้ว lab ไม่ improve → reconsider TTP/HUS.

PEARL 4 — Aspirin 81 mg ต้องเริ่ม <16 wk เพื่อ Prevention Effect จะ Maximal
Roberge 2017 meta-analysis: aspirin started before 16 wk → maximal PE prevention. เริ่มหลัง 16 wk = ↓ benefit อย่างมีนัยสำคัญ. ดังนั้น: ใน booking visit (<16 wk) ต้องประเมิน risk factors (prior PE, chronic HTN, DM, APS, obesity, nulliparity, age ≥35) และเริ่ม aspirin 81 mg/day ทันที. ใน NICU: ถ้าแม่คลอดก่อนกำหนดจาก PE → review ว่าได้ aspirin หรือเปล่า — ข้อมูล prevention สำหรับ next pregnancy.

PEARL 5 — ACEi/ARB ต้อง Stop ก่อน Conception — ไม่ใช่แค่ 'เมื่อรู้ว่าท้อง'
ACEi/ARB: 1st trimester → CNS + cardiac anomalies; 2nd/3rd trimester → fetal renal failure + oligohydramnios + pulmonary hypoplasia + skull ossification defects (Cooper NEJM 2006). ACOG: stop even in preconception period. ดังนั้น: ถ้า neonate มีประวัติ maternal ACEi exposure → ประเมิน renal function (BUN/Cr), urine output, renal US; pulmonary function ถ้า oligohydramnios. ใน NICU พบ: anuria, oliguria, renal tubular dysgenesis — rare แต่ severe.
13. Evidence Update — Key Studies
ACOG Practice Bulletin No. 202. Obstet Gynecol 2019;133:e1: gestational hypertension and preeclampsia; classification + management guidelines; 'preeclampsia without severe features' replaces 'mild'
ACOG Practice Bulletin No. 203. Obstet Gynecol 2019;133:215: chronic hypertension in pregnancy; management and BP targets
Tita AT et al NEJM 2022;387:664 (CHAP Trial): mild chronic HTN in pregnancy; tight control <140/90 → fewer adverse outcomes, no ↑ SGA; current standard of care
Gyamfi-Bannerman C et al NEJM 2016;374:1311: antenatal betamethasone 34–36+6 wk → ↓ respiratory morbidity; benefit for late preterm delivery
Duley L et al Cochrane 2010 (MgSO₄): 6 trials 11,444 women; MgSO₄ vs placebo → seizure RR 0.41 (0.29–0.58); superior to phenytoin/diazepam
Doyle LW et al Cochrane 2009 (MgSO₄ neuroprotection): MgSO₄ before anticipated early preterm → ↓ cerebral palsy in surviving infants; ACOG recommends <32 wk
Henderson JT et al JAMA 2021;326:1192 (USPSTF aspirin): updated evidence: aspirin RR 0.85 PE, RR 0.80 preterm birth, RR 0.82 IUGR; no bleeding harms
Duley L et al Cochrane 2019 (antiplatelet + PE): 74 trials >40,000 women; aspirin → ↓ PE, ↓ fetal/neonatal death, ↓ preterm <37 wk, ↓ SGA
Roberge S et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 2017;216:110 (aspirin dose/timing): aspirin started <16 wk → maximal benefit; evidence for 100–150 mg dose over 81 mg
Hofmeyr GJ et al Cochrane 2018 (calcium): 13 trials 15,730 women; ↓ PE risk RR 0.45 (0.31–0.65); greatest benefit low-baseline-calcium populations
Rumbold A et al Cochrane 2008 (vitamins C+E): 15 trials >20,000 women; vitamins C+E → no benefit for PE prevention (RR 0.94)
Levine RJ et al NEJM 2004;350:672: circulating sFlt-1 ↑ + PlGF ↓ weeks before clinical preeclampsia; angiogenic biomarker concept
Venkatesha S et al Nature Med 2006;12:642: soluble endoglin potentiates severe PE + HELLP + FGR in rats; pathogenic role confirmed in vivo
Sibai BM Am J Obstet Gynecol 2011;205:191: expectant management severe PE <34 wk: criteria, outcomes; pregnancy prolongation + ↓ RDS; maternal safety acceptable in tertiary center
Odendaal HJ et al Obstet Gynecol 1990;76:1070: aggressive vs expectant management severe PE 28–34 wk: expectant → significant prolongation + ↓ RDS
Poon LC et al Hypertension 2009;53:812: combined first-trimester screening: PlGF + uterine artery PI + MAP + PAPP-A → 93% detection early-onset PE at 5% FPR
Cooper WO et al NEJM 2006;354:2443: ACEi first-trimester exposure → major congenital malformations CNS + cardiac; teratogenicity confirmed
Davis EF et al Pediatrics 2012;129:e1552: systematic review 18 studies 45,249 individuals: ↑ BP + BMI in children/young adults born to preeclamptic pregnancies (DOHaD link)
Benschop L et al Heart 2019;105:1273: future CV risk after hypertensive disorder of pregnancy: ↑ HTN, dyslipidemia, endothelial dysfunction years later
14. Self-Check Q&A
Q: Preeclampsia 'without severe features' หมายความว่าอะไร? ต่างจาก 'with severe features' อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Without severe features: BP 140–159/90–109 + proteinuria/end-organ criteria แต่ไม่มี severe features. With severe features: BP ≥160/110; OR thrombocytopenia <100k; OR LFT 2x ULN + RUQ pain; OR progressive renal insufficiency; OR pulmonary edema; OR new headache. สำคัญ: progression unpredictable within hours — vigilance เสมอ; 'without severe features' ≠ safe ตลอดไป.
Q: Two-stage pathophysiology ของ preeclampsia คืออะไร? Stage I และ II แตกต่างกันอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Stage I: incomplete trophoblast invasion → spiral arteries remain high-resistance → ↓ placental perfusion. Stage II: placental ischemia → release sFlt-1/soluble endoglin/cytokines → systemic endothelial dysfunction → HTN/proteinuria/multiorgan damage. Pre-existing vascular disease (DM/chronic HTN/SLE) → worsens both stages.
Q: MgSO₄: dose, duration, toxicity monitoring, และ neonatal effects?

ตอบ: Dose: 4 g IV loading → 1–2 g/h maintenance; ↓ dose in renal failure. Duration: labor + 24–48h postpartum. Toxicity: ↓ reflexes → respiratory depression → cardiac arrest (toxic levels). Neonatal: cord Mg ≈ maternal; respiratory depression + poor tone + hypothermia; neonatology team in delivery room; calcium gluconate as antidote if severe neonatal Mg toxicity.
Q: HELLP ต่างจาก TTP/HUS อย่างไร? ทำไม treatment จึงต่างกัน?

ตอบ: HELLP: variant of PE; hemolysis + ↑ LFT + ↓ platelets; treatment = delivery (+ supports); resolves post-delivery. TTP: ↓ ADAMTS13; neurologic dominant; treatment = plasmapheresis. HUS: renal dominant; complement/Shiga toxin; treatment = plasmapheresis. Delivery ไม่รักษา TTP/HUS — diagnosis essential ก่อน proceed.
Q: CHAP trial คืออะไร? เปลี่ยน practice อย่างไร?

ตอบ: CHAP (Tita NEJM 2022): pragmatic RCT mild chronic HTN in pregnancy; tight control <140/90 vs treat only if BP ≥160/105. Result: tight control → fewer adverse outcomes; NO increase SGA. Current practice: target BP <140/90 using labetalol or nifedipine XL as first-line. Disproved concern that tight control → fetal compromise.
Q: Aspirin prevention ของ preeclampsia: indications, dose, timing?

ตอบ: High-risk indications (ACOG/USPSTF): prior PE, pregestational DM, chronic HTN, multifetal gestation, renal disease, APS/SLE. 2+ moderate factors (nulliparity, obesity, family history, age ≥35). Dose: 81 mg/day (emerging evidence 100–162 mg more effective); start ideally 12 wk, must be <16 wk; continue throughout pregnancy. Starting after 16 wk → ↓ benefit significantly (Roberge 2017).
Q: ACE inhibitor exposure ใน 2nd/3rd trimester → neonatal evaluation ต้องทำอะไรบ้าง?

ตอบ: ACEi/ARB 2nd/3rd trimester → fetal renal failure, oligohydramnios (→ pulmonary hypoplasia, limb contractures, skull ossification defects). Neonate: monitor urine output + BUN/Cr; renal US; respiratory assessment (oligohydramnios → pulmonary hypoplasia); blood pressure. Renal tubular dysgenesis ← rare severe complication.
Q: Neonatologist ควรรู้อะไรเกี่ยวกับ corticosteroid ใน preeclampsia?

ตอบ: Betamethasone 12 mg IM q24h x2 doses: standard for <34 wk; ↓ RDS, IVH, NEC, mortality. Late preterm 34 0/7 – 36 6/7 wk: benefit demonstrated (Gyamfi-Bannerman NEJM 2016). ต้องรู้ว่าแม่ได้ครบ 2 doses หรือไม่ และ timing ก่อนคลอด — <24h from first dose = incomplete benefit; document ใน NICU admission note ทุกราย.
Q: Postpartum preeclampsia คืออะไร และเกิดตอนไหน?

ตอบ: Preeclampsia ที่ diagnosed ครั้งแรกหลัง delivery — usually within 2 wk postpartum. อาจไม่มี antepartum diagnosis. ต้องมี high clinical suspicion ในสตรีที่ BP ↑ หลังคลอด + headache/visual changes + edema. Clinical management: same as antepartum PE; MgSO₄ if severe features; antihypertensives; close monitoring.
Q: Calcium ป้องกัน preeclampsia ได้จริงไหม? ประชากรไหนได้ประโยชน์มากที่สุด?

ตอบ: Cochrane 2018 (Hofmeyr): calcium 1.5–2 g/day → ↓ PE risk RR 0.45 (0.31–0.65). Greatest benefit: low baseline calcium intake (RR 0.36) — ประชากรในประเทศกำลังพัฒนา หรือ ประเทศที่มี dairy consumption ต่ำ (รวมถึงไทย — น่าสนใจ). US trial (calcium 2g/day ใน nulliparous): ไม่ได้ผล — ประชากร US มี calcium intake เพียงพออยู่แล้ว.

Day 114 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.17
บทต่อไป: Chapter 17 — Pregnancy Complicated by Diabetes Mellitus. เตรียมตัว: classification DM in pregnancy (pregestational vs GDM); pathophysiology fetal macrosomia + neonatal hypoglycemia; IDM syndrome; congenital anomalies (caudal regression syndrome); neonatal hypoglycemia management in IDM vs SGA; cardiomyopathy of IDM; polycythemia; jaundice; respiratory distress; long-term metabolic outcomes of IDM (obesity/T2DM).
